### 1. PACKAGE.JSON - Dependencies & Scripts

- `next-intl` (4.8.4) - Internationalization (i18n)

### 2. Testing Setup

**Vitest Config** (`vitest.config.ts`):

---

### 3. Linting & Formatting

**ESLint Configuration** (`eslint.config.mjs`):
- Uses ESLint 9 flat config (new format)
- Extends:
  - `eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals` - Next.js best practices
  - `eslint-config-next/typescript` - TypeScript support
- Global ignores: `.next/`, `out/`, `build/`, `next-env.d.ts`
- No Prettier config found (may be using ESLint for formatting only)

**TypeScript Config** (`tsconfig.json`):
- Target: ES2017

- JSX: react-jsx (automatic)
- Isolated modules, incremental compilation

---

### 4. CI/CD & Deployment

**No GitHub Actions Workflows Found** in the root `.github/` directory. This suggests:
- Deploying directly to Vercel (Vercel automatically handles CI/CD)
- Vercel integration is implicit via `next.config.ts`

**Vercel Configuration Indicators:**
- `next.config.ts` uses Vercel optimizations
- `.gitignore` includes `.vercel/`
- Environment variables likely managed on Vercel dashboard
- Production database: Turso (Vercel partnership)

---

### 5. Performance Optimizations

**Image Optimization:**
- No `next/image` components detected in search
- Using standard HTML for images (may optimize later)

**Caching Strategy:**
- Quotes use `revalidatePath()` after mutations (cache invalidation)
- No ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration) detected
- In-memory rate limiter with sliding window (non-distributed)

**Database:**
- Turso (libsql) - SQLite for edge
- Supports both local (`file:local.db`) and remote (`DATABASE_URL`)
- Connection pooling via `@libsql/client`

**PDF Generation:**
- Configured as `serverExternalPackage` in `next.config.ts`
- Runs server-side only

---

### 6. Security Measures

**Authentication:**
```
/src/features/auth/lib/auth.ts - Session-based auth
- Password hashing: bcryptjs (not plain-text)
- Session cookie: pragma_admin_session (httpOnly, secure, sameSite: lax)
- 24-hour session expiry
- CSRF: Handled automatically by Next.js Server Actions (Origin header validation)
```

**Rate Limiting:**
- Location: `src/shared/lib/rate-limit.ts`
- Implementation: In-memory Map with sliding window
- **Caveat:** Works for single-process; on Vercel (serverless), each instance has its own store
  - Recommendation in code: Use Upstash Redis for global rate limiting
- **Applied to:**
  - Login endpoint: 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP
  - PDF download: 30 requests per 60 seconds per IP

**Input Validation:**
- Zod schemas for all forms: `src/features/quotes/lib/schemas.ts`
- Schema validation includes:
  - Email format validation
  - String min-length checks
  - Enum restrictions (currency, locale, status)
  - Number range validation (discount 0-100)

**CSRF Protection:**
- Next.js Server Actions provide automatic CSRF protection
- Explicitly noted in `src/features/auth/components/loginAction.ts`
- No manual CSRF tokens needed

---

## COMPREHENSIVE PROJECT EXPLORATION REPORT

### PRAGMA Web - Next.js 16 AI Solutions Landing Page + Quote Management System

---

## 1. ROUTES & PAGE STRUCTURE

### Root App Structure
- **Root page** (`/src/app/page.tsx`): Redirects to `/en` (locale routing)
- **Root layout** (`/src/app/layout.tsx`): Sets up Google Fonts (Space Grotesk for display, Inter for sans, JetBrains Mono for monospace), metadata, CSS variables

### Locale-Based Routing (EN + ES)
All routes are under `[locale]` parameter supporting `/en` and `/es`:

#### Landing Page Routes
- **`/[locale]/`** - Main homepage
  - Components: Hero, Services, About, CaseStudies, Testimonials, Contact
  - Navbar (sticky, fixed position)
  - Footer
  - Full SEO with JSON-LD schema (Organization + WebSite)
  - Responsive design with mobile menu

#### Admin Panel Routes (Protected by password auth)
- **`/[locale]/admin`** - Admin dashboard
  - Lists all quotes in a responsive table (desktop table / mobile cards)
  - Status badges: `draft`, `sent`, `accepted`, `rejected`
  - Actions: Copy link, Edit, Delete
  - Quote type display: `line-items` or `blueprint`
  - Header with "New Quote" button + Logout

- **`/[locale]/admin/quotes/new`** - Create new quote
  - QuoteForm component (comprehensive form with two quote types)
  - Supports both `line-items` and `blueprint` quote types

- **`/[locale]/admin/quotes/[id]/edit`** - Edit existing quote
  - Same QuoteForm, pre-populated with existing quote data

#### Public Quote Viewing
- **`/[locale]/quote/[id]`** - Public quote viewer (shareable link)
  - QuoteViewer component
  - Language toggle (EN/ES)
  - Actions: Download PDF, Accept Quote
  - Displays quote data (client, items, pricing, status)

#### API Routes
- **`GET /api/quote/[id]/pdf`** - PDF generation endpoint
  - Uses `@react-pdf/renderer` for React-to-PDF conversion
  - Rate-limited (30 requests per 60 seconds per IP)
  - Returns branded PDF with dynamic filename

- **`GET /api/dev/seed`** - Development database seeder
  - Disabled in production (`dynamic = "force-dynamic"`)
  - Populates with sample quotes in multiple currencies (MXN, USD)

#### SEO Routes
- **`/robots.txt`** - Disallows `/admin/` and `/quote/` (private pages)
- **`/sitemap.ts`** - XML sitemap with `/en` and `/es` entries

---

## 2. LANDING PAGE SECTIONS & COMPONENTS

**Location**: `/src/features/landing/components/`

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Hero** | Full-height hero with animated title, subtitle, CTA buttons, scroll indicator. Uses framer-motion animations. |
| **Services** | 6-card grid: AI Consulting, Custom AI, Automation, Analytics, Web Dev, UI/UX. Hover effects, staggered animations. |
| **About** | "Why PRAGMA" section with differentiators (Results-Driven, Full-Stack, Transparent). Includes stat blocks (Projects, Clients, Years). |
| **CaseStudies** | 3 case study cards with client logos, descriptions, results. Linked to external resources. |
| **Testimonials** | Client testimonials with avatars and quotes. |
| **Contact** | Contact form + info cards (Email, Location). Form submission via server action, toast notifications. |

**Design System**:
- Floating accent orbs (animated with blur and scale)
- Dot grid background pattern
- Gradient mesh overlays
- Staggered animations on scroll
- Responsive mobile-first layout

---

## 3. ADMIN PANEL - COMPREHENSIVE BREAKDOWN

### Authentication
- **Single admin password** auth (bcrypt-based)
- Session cookie: `pragma_admin_session` (httpOnly, 24-hour expiry)
- Rate limiting: 5 login attempts per IP per 15 minutes
- Protected by middleware + server-side check in admin layout

**Location**: `/src/features/auth/`
- `lib/auth.ts`: Session management (bcrypt, cookies)
- `components/LoginForm.tsx`: Login UI
- `components/LogoutButton.tsx`: Logout action
- `components/loginAction.ts`: Server action for login

### Quote Management Dashboard

**Dashboard View** (`/admin`):
- Table/grid listing all quotes sorted by creation date (newest first)
- Columns: Title, Client, Company, Status Badge, Quote Type, Amount, Actions
- Mobile: Card layout with essential info
- Actions per quote: Copy Link, Edit, Delete
- Create new quote button

**Quote Table Component**:
- Responsive design (desktop table, mobile cards)
- Copy-to-clipboard for shareable links
- Confirm dialog for deletion
- Status color coding

### Quote Form (Create & Edit)

**Location**: `/src/features/quotes/components/QuoteForm.tsx`

**Two Quote Types**:

1. **Line-Items Quote** (default)
   - Itemized pricing table
   - Fields: Description, Quantity, Unit Price (auto-calculates totals)
   - Dynamic add/remove line items
   - Subtotal, Discount %, Total calculations
   
2. **Blueprint Quote** (narrative proposal)
   - Problem statement
   - Opportunity
   - Deliverables (numbered list)
   - Timeline
   - Fixed Price / List Price
   - Preferential Note
   - Payment Terms
   - Next Steps (array)
   - Scope Note

**Common Fields** (both types):
- Client Name, Email, Company
- Quote Title, Description
- Currency (USD, EUR, MXN, COP)
- Notes
- Valid Until (date)
- Status (draft, sent, accepted, rejected)
- Locale (en, es)

**Form Validation**:
- Uses Zod schemas (`/src/features/quotes/lib/schemas.ts`)
- Field-level error display
- Prevents submission with invalid data

---

## 4. QUOTE SYSTEM - DETAILED FLOW

### Quote Types & Data Model

**Quote Interface** (`/src/features/quotes/types/index.ts`):
```
- id: string (nanoid, 10 chars)
- clientName, clientEmail, clientCompany: string
- title, description: string
- quoteType: "line-items" | "blueprint"
- lineItems: { id, description, quantity, unitPrice }[]
- currency: "USD" | "EUR" | "MXN" | "COP"
- discount: number (percentage)
- status: "draft" | "sent" | "accepted" | "rejected"
- validUntil: string (date)
- locale: "en" | "es"
- notes: string
- [Blueprint-only]: problem, opportunity, deliverables[], timeline, fixedPrice, listPrice, preferentialNote, paymentTerms, nextSteps[], scopeNote
- createdAt, updatedAt: ISO timestamps
```

### Quote Lifecycle

1. **Create**: Admin fills form → `createQuoteAction` server action → DB insert → Redirect to admin
2. **Edit**: Admin opens existing quote → Form pre-populates → `updateQuoteAction` → DB update
3. **Share**: Admin copies link → `/{locale}/quote/{id}` → Public viewer (no auth required)
4. **Accept**: Client views quote → Clicks "Accept" → `acceptQuoteAction` → Status changes to "accepted"
5. **PDF Export**: Client/Admin downloads PDF → `/api/quote/{id}/pdf` → React-PDF renders + streams

### Database (Turso / LibSQL)

**Schema** (`/src/shared/lib/db.ts`):
- Table: `quotes` (single table, JSON-serialized complex fields)
- Columns: id, client_*, title, description, quote_type, line_items (JSON), currency, discount, status, valid_until, locale, notes, [blueprint fields as JSON or primitives], created_at, updated_at
- Table: `contact_submissions` (from landing page contact form)

**Database Queries** (`/src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts`):
- `getAllQuotes()`: SELECT * ORDER BY created_at DESC
- `getQuoteById(id)`: SELECT where id = ?
- `createQuote(data)`: INSERT
- `updateQuote(id, data)`: UPDATE
- `updateQuoteStatus(id, status)`: UPDATE status only
- `deleteQuote(id)`: DELETE

### PDF Generation

- **Endpoint**: `GET /api/quote/{id}/pdf`
- **Library**: `@react-pdf/renderer`
- **Component**: `QuotePdfDocument` (React component that renders as PDF)
- **Output**: Branded PDF with PRAGMA logo, quote details, itemized table/blueprint layout, footer
- **Filename**: `pragma-{clientSlug}-{titleSlug}.pdf`
- **Rate Limiting**: 30 requests per 60 seconds per IP

---

## 5. UI COMPONENT LIBRARY

**Location**: `/src/shared/components/ui/`

### Custom Components (Tailwind + Framer Motion)

| Component | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| **Button** | Variants: primary (cyan glow), secondary (border), ghost, danger, success. Sizes: sm, md, lg. Uses forwardRef. |
| **Card** | Surface/border styling, optional hover effect with glow. Supports dark backgrounds. |
| **Badge** | Status indicators: default, cyan, green, red. Inline badges for tags. |
| **Input** | Text input with placeholder, error states. Matches design system. |
| **Textarea** | Multi-line input, same styling as Input. |
| **Select** | Dropdown select for currencies, quote types, statuses. |
| **AnimateIn** | Wrapper component with staggered fade-in/slide-up animations (configurable delay). |
| **Toaster** | Toast notifications system (success, error states). |

### Layout Components

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **Navbar** | Fixed header, sticky nav with logo, desktop nav links, mobile hamburger, language toggle. |
| **Footer** | Footer with links, tagline, social icons. |
| **LanguageToggle** | EN/ES language switcher. Uses `next-intl` navigation. |

### SEO Component

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **JsonLd** | Renders `<script type="application/ld+json">` tags for schema.org structured data. |

---

## 6. DESIGN SYSTEM & STYLING

**Tech Stack**: Tailwind CSS v4 + CSS-in-JS theme

**Color Palette** (`globals.css`):
```
--color-pragma-bg:       #0a0a0f  (very dark blue)
--color-pragma-surface:  #12121a  (dark blue, elevated)
--color-pragma-border:   #1e1e2e  (dark purple-gray)
--color-pragma-accent:   #00f0ff  (cyan, primary CTA)
--color-pragma-accent-2: #7c3aed  (purple, secondary)
--color-pragma-accent-3: #00ff88  (green, success)
--color-pragma-text:     #e4e4e7  (light gray)
--color-pragma-muted:    #71717a  (medium gray)
```

**Typography**:
- `--font-display`: Space Grotesk (headings, large text)
- `--font-sans`: Inter (body, UI text)
- `--font-mono`: JetBrains Mono (code, IDs)

**Animations** (defined in `globals.css`):
- `glow-pulse`: 3s opacity pulse (used on decorative orbs)
- `float`: 6s translate-Y bounce (floating elements)
- `fade-in`: 0.6s opacity fade
- `slide-up`: 0.6s opacity + translateY

**Visual Effects**:
- Dot grid background (30px spacing, radial-gradient)
- Gradient mesh (3-layer ellipse blend of cyan, purple, green)
- Glow effects (box-shadow with rgba color stops)
- Backdrop blur (frosted glass effect)
- Custom scrollbar (6px width, pragma-border color)

---

## 7. INTERNATIONALIZATION (i18n)

**Framework**: `next-intl` v4.8.4

**Routing Configuration** (`/src/i18n/routing.ts`):
- Locales: `en`, `es`
- Default: `en`

**Middleware** (`middleware.ts`):
- Intercepts all requests
- Routes locale-prefixed paths: `/en/*`, `/es/*`
- Matches any path without API/file extensions
- Handles locale detection + redirection

**Message Files** (`/src/messages/`):
- `en.json`: English translations
- `es.json`: Spanish translations

**Translation Keys** (nested structure in JSON):
- `Navbar`: Navigation labels
- `Hero`: Hero section copy
- `Services`: Service cards + descriptions
- `About`: About section + differentiators
- `CaseStudies`: Case study titles/descriptions
- `Testimonials`: Testimonial data
- `Contact`: Form labels, messages
- `Footer`: Footer copy
- `Admin`: Admin panel strings
- `Quote`: Quote status labels, form placeholders
- `Auth`: Login/logout messages

**Usage in Components**:
- Server: `const t = await getTranslations("SectionName")`
- Client: `const t = useTranslations("SectionName")`
- Navigation: `Link` component (automatically handles locale in URL)

---

## 8. AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION

### Session Management
- **Cookie-based**: `pragma_admin_session` (httpOnly, secure, SameSite: Lax)
- **Duration**: 24 hours
- **Verification**: Simple presence check (no JWT/token validation needed)

### Password Security
- **Algorithm**: bcrypt with salt rounds
- **Hash Storage**: Environment variable `ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH` (never plain text)
- **Generation**: `node scripts/hash-password.mjs <password>`

### Rate Limiting
- **Login endpoint**: 5 attempts per IP per 15 minutes
- **PDF endpoint**: 30 requests per IP per 60 seconds
- **Implementation**: In-memory cache with TTL (not suitable for multi-process, would need Redis in production)

### Protected Routes
- `/[locale]/admin/*` - Requires valid session cookie
- Admin layout checks `isAuthenticated()` before rendering
- Redirects to LoginForm if not authenticated

### Server Actions (CSRF Protection)
- Built-in Next.js CSRF protection via Origin header validation
- All form submissions use server actions (`"use server"`)
- No explicit CSRF token needed

---

## 9. TECH STACK SUMMARY

### Core Framework
- **Next.js 16.2.2** (App Router, Server Components, Server Actions)
- **React 19.2.4**
- **TypeScript 5**

### Styling & UI
- **Tailwind CSS v4** (with `@tailwindcss/postcss`)
- **Lucide React** (icons, 1.7.0)
- **Framer Motion** (animations, 12.38.0)

### Data & State
- **Turso / LibSQL** (`@libsql/client` v0.17.2) - SQLite-compatible serverless database
- **Zod** (v4.3.6) - Schema validation
- **nanoid** (v5.1.7) - ID generation

### PDF Generation
- **@react-pdf/renderer** (v4.3.2) - React-to-PDF

### Internationalization
- **next-intl** (v4.8.4) - Multi-language routing & translation

### Authentication
- **bcryptjs** (v3.0.3) - Password hashing

### Testing & Code Quality
- **Vitest** (v4.1.2) - Unit & integration tests
- **Playwright** (v1.59.1) - E2E tests
- **ESLint** (v9) - Code linting
- **TypeScript** (v5) - Type checking

### Development Tools
- **Node.js** (scripts for seeding, password hashing)
- **PostCSS** (CSS processing)

---

## 10. FILE STRUCTURE OVERVIEW

## 11. KEY FEATURES & CAPABILITIES

### Landing Page
- SEO-optimized with JSON-LD schema
- Responsive design (mobile-first)
- Smooth animations on scroll
- Language toggle (EN/ES)
- Contact form with validation
- Case studies showcase
- Testimonials section

### Quote Management
- Two quote types (line-items, blueprint)
- Full CRUD operations
- Status tracking (draft, sent, accepted, rejected)
- Multi-currency support (USD, EUR, MXN, COP)
- Automatic calculations (subtotal, discount, total)
- PDF export with branding
- Shareable links
- Client-side form validation + server-side Zod validation

### Admin Panel
- Protected by password auth
- Quote dashboard with filtering/sorting
- One-click quote links (copy to clipboard)
- Edit/Delete functionality
- Quick create new quote

### PDF Generation
- React-to-PDF rendering
- Branded template with PRAGMA logo
- Dynamic filename generation
- Rate-limited to prevent abuse

<result>Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive summary report:

## Comprehensive Backend, Data Layer & Infrastructure Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the Next.js project, here is the complete backend picture:

### 1. **DATABASE SCHEMA**

**Provider: LibSQL/Turso**
- Engine: SQLite-compatible via `@libsql/client`
- Two main tables initialized in `/src/shared/lib/db.ts`:

**QUOTES Table** (45 columns):
- Core fields: `id`, `client_name`, `client_email`, `client_company`, `title`, `description`
- Quote type: `quote_type` (enum: "line-items" | "blueprint")
- Pricing: `line_items` (JSON array), `currency`, `discount`, `notes`
- Status: `status` (enum: "draft" | "sent" | "accepted" | "rejected")
- Validity: `valid_until`, `locale` (en/es)
- Blueprint-specific fields: `problem`, `opportunity`, `deliverables` (JSON), `timeline`, `fixed_price`, `list_price`, `preferential_note`, `payment_terms`, `next_steps` (JSON array), `scope_note`
- Timestamps: `created_at`, `updated_at`

**CONTACT_SUBMISSIONS Table** (4 columns):
- `id`, `name`, `email`, `company`, `message`, `created_at`

**Database Initialization:**
- `initDb()` creates tables with `IF NOT EXISTS` for idempotency
- Includes migration system for adding new columns to existing installations (catches exceptions for already-existing columns)

---

### 2. **SERVER ACTIONS & API ROUTES**

**Quote Management (Server Actions in `/src/features/quotes/actions/index.ts`):**
- `createQuoteAction(formData)` - Creates quote, validates with Zod schema, revalidates admin path
- `updateQuoteAction(id, formData)` - Partial quote updates, revalidates
- `deleteQuoteAction(id)` - Deletes quote, revalidates
- `acceptQuoteAction(id)` - Updates quote status to "accepted", revalidates quote view

**Quote Queries (`/src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts`):**
- `getAllQuotes()` - Returns all quotes ordered by `created_at DESC`
- `getQuoteById(id)` - Fetches single quote with parameterized queries
- `createQuote(data)` - Inserts with nanoid(10) as ID
- `updateQuote(id, data)` - Full/partial updates with timestamp
- `updateQuoteStatus(id, status)` - Status-only updates
- `deleteQuote(id)` - Soft/hard delete (appears to be hard)

**Landing/Contact (Server Actions in `/src/features/landing/actions/index.ts`):**
- `submitContactAction(formData)` - Stores contact form submissions in DB (no email sending)

**API Routes:**
- `GET /api/quote/[id]/pdf` - Renders quote to PDF using `@react-pdf/renderer`, implements rate limiting (30 req/min), returns downloadable PDF with filename pattern `pragma-{clientSlug}-{titleSlug}.pdf`
- `GET /api/dev/seed` - Development endpoint that populates 4 sample quotes (only works in non-production)

---

### 3. **AUTHENTICATION SYSTEM**

**Location:** `/src/features/auth/`

**Authentication Method:**
- **Simple cookie-based authentication** (custom implementation, NOT Auth.js)
- Password hash: BCrypt comparison against `process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH`
- Session token: Random UUID stored in HTTP-only cookie `pragma_admin_session`
- Session duration: 24 hours max age

**Key Functions (`/src/features/auth/lib/auth.ts`):**
- `verifyPassword(password)` - Compares input against bcrypt hash
- `createSession()` - Sets HTTP-only, secure (prod only), SameSite=lax cookie
- `isAuthenticated()` - Checks if session cookie exists
- `destroySession()` - Deletes session cookie on logout

**Server Actions (`/src/features/auth/components/loginAction.ts`):**
- `loginAction(formData)` - Validates password, implements rate limiting (5 attempts / 15 min per IP), creates session, redirects to `/en/admin`
- `logoutAction()` - Destroys session, redirects to `/en`

**Admin Access Control:**
- Admin layout (`/src/app/[locale]/admin/layout.tsx`) checks `isAuthenticated()` before rendering protected pages
- Shows LoginForm if unauthenticated
- No role-based access control (all authenticated users = full admin access)

**Rate Limiting on Auth:**
- 5 login attempts per 15 minutes per IP
- IP detection: X-Forwarded-For header (proxies), X-Real-IP fallback

---

### 4. **EMAIL & NOTIFICATIONS**

**Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED**
- No email sending dependencies installed
- Contact submissions are stored to database but never emailed
- No webhook integrations for notifications
- No scheduled jobs for sending quote reminders

---

### 5. **THIRD-PARTY INTEGRATIONS**

**Confirmed Integrations:**
1. **LibSQL/Turso** - Database provider via `@libsql/client`
2. **React PDF** (`@react-pdf/renderer`) - PDF generation for quote exports
3. **Next-intl** - i18n support (English/Spanish)
4. **Zod** - Schema validation for quote forms
5. **Tailwind CSS** - Styling via Tailwind v4
6. **Framer Motion** - Animation library (in dependencies)
7. **Lucide React** - Icon library
8. **Playwright** - E2E testing (dev dependency)
9. **Vitest** - Unit testing framework

**NOT Present:**
- No payment processing (Stripe, Paddle, etc.)
- No CRM integration
- No email service (SendGrid, Resend, etc.)
- No authentication middleware (Clerk, NextAuth, etc.)
- No analytics SDK
- No monitoring/logging service (Sentry, LogRocket)

---

### 6. **ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES**

Required configuration (inferred from code):
- `DATABASE_URL` - LibSQL connection string (defaults to `file:local.db` for local dev)
- `DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN` - LibSQL auth token (optional, undefined if not provided)
- `ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH` - BCrypt hash of admin password (required for auth)
- `NODE_ENV` - Production flag for secure cookies

---

### 7. **DATABASE PROVIDER DETAILS**

**LibSQL/Turso Setup:**
```typescript
createClient({ url: DATABASE_URL, authToken: DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN })
```
- Client is singleton cached: `client` instance persists across requests
- Parameterized queries: Uses `{ sql, args }` pattern for SQL injection prevention
- Database initialization happens on first operation (lazy initialization)
- Supports local SQLite (`file:local.db`) or remote Turso (via `DATABASE_URL`)

---

### 8. **DEPLOYMENT CONFIG**

**Next.js Config (`/next.config.ts`):**
- Next.js 16.2.2
- Integrated with `next-intl` plugin for i18n
- External packages: `@react-pdf/renderer` marked as server-external (not bundled)

**Middleware (`/middleware.ts`):**
- Routing middleware via `next-intl` for locale handling
- Intercepts requests to inject locale context
- Matcher excludes API routes, `_next`, static files

**Vercel Integration:**
- Project linked to Vercel (project.json contains projectId, orgId)
- Ready for Vercel deployment
- No `vercel.json` config file (uses defaults)

**Build Scripts:**
- `npm run dev` - Next.js dev server
- `npm run build` - Production build
- `npm run start` - Start production server
- `npm run db:seed` - Node script for DB seeding

---

### 9. **BACKEND GAPS & MISSING FEATURES**

**Critical Gaps:**
1. **NO Email Sending** - Contact forms and quote notifications have no email capability
2. **NO Quote Notifications** - When quotes are updated/sent, no client notifications
3. **NO Audit Logging** - No tracking of who made what changes when
4. **NO Quote Signing/E-Signature** - Acceptance is stored but no formal signing
5. **NO Payment Integration** - Cannot accept payments through quotes
6. **NO API Authentication** - PDF endpoint has no auth (public, rate-limited only)
7. **NO Data Validation on API** - Rate limiting but no schema validation on PDF route
8. **Single Admin User** - One password hash, no multi-user support
9. **In-Memory Rate Limiting** - Works only per-process instance; breaks on serverless (each instance has its own limit)
10. **NO Database Backups Configured** - SQLite depends on Turso backup strategy
11. **NO Error Monitoring** - No Sentry/monitoring for production errors
12. **NO Search/Filtering** - Admin dashboard shows all quotes, no pagination or search
13. **NO Permissions/Roles** - All authenticated users have full admin access
14. **NO Quote Templates** - Must fill out form for each quote

---

### 10. **DATA MODEL SUMMARY**

**Quote Types:**
- **Line-items**: Traditional invoice-style quotes with itemized list + total
- **Blueprint**: Consultative quotes with problem/opportunity/deliverables/timeline/fixed pricing

**Quote Lifecycle:**
- Draft → Sent → Accepted | Rejected
- Storage: JSON-serialized for nested structures (lineItems, deliverables, nextSteps)

**Localization:**
- Stored per-quote: `locale` field (en/es)
- Middleware routes all traffic through locale prefix (`/en/`, `/es/`)

---

### File Paths for Reference

**Core Backend Files:**
- `/src/shared/lib/db.ts` - Database client and schema
- `/src/shared/lib/rate-limit.ts` - Rate limiting utility
- `/src/features/quotes/lib/queries.ts` - Quote CRUD operations
- `/src/features/quotes/lib/schemas.ts` - Zod validation schemas
- `/src/features/auth/lib/auth.ts` - Authentication logic
- `/src/app/api/quote/[id]/pdf/route.ts` - PDF generation endpoint
- `/src/app/api/dev/seed/route.ts` - Seed data endpoint
- `/src/features/landing/actions/index.ts` - Contact form action
- `/src/features/auth/components/loginAction.ts` - Login/logout actions
- `/middleware.ts` - i18n routing middleware
- `/next.config.ts` - Next.js configuration
- `/package.json` - Dependencies

This is a **lean, single-user quote generation tool** with basic authentication, focused on creating and sharing quote PDFs. The backend is minimal and focused—no multi-user features, no email, no payment processing.</result>
